topic: Comparative analysis of open-source LLM evaluation frameworks for RAG hallucination detection
question: How do the top three open-source LLM evaluation frameworks (HELM, DeepEval, and RAGAS) compare in their ability to detect hallucinations in RAG pipelines as of early 2026?

pico_breakdown.intervention: Ability to detect hallucinations in RAG pipelines.
pico_breakdown.comparator: Comparative analysis between HELM, DeepEval, and RAGAS.
pico_breakdown.outcome: Accuracy metrics, ease of integration, and latency benchmarks.

must_include: Comparison of metric types (model-based vs. deterministic)
must_exclude: Closed-source/proprietary evaluation services (e.g., Giskard SaaS, Weights & Biases proprietary features)

output_requirements.format: Full report with citations and a technical comparative table
output_requirements.audience: Senior Machine Learning Engineers and Technical Architects

prioritise: Technical engineering blogs from reputable AI labs (e.g., Anthropic, Meta, Mistral)
